title: Max (bunny)
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Max}} {{Character |type=Bounty hunter |image=[[File:Max.jpg]] |name=Max |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Bunny]]''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' |gender= |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|White]]''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' |feathers= |eyes=Black |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|He's a bunny, I'm a dog. We're dangerous, but we work cheap.|Max's partner|}} '''Max''' was a demented, diminutive, and destructive member of the [[bunny]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. Over the course of his career, he worked with several different beings, such as a [[Unidentified sentient dog|dog]] and [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] agent [[Kyle Katarn]]. His visage could be found in various locations across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], notably in [[Fuel City]] on the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]]. On that same planet, Max stayed at the [[House of Max]], in the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Barons Hed]]. Later, he teamed up with Katarn to hunt down [[Derrida/Legends|Derrida]], a [[Ketton/Legends|Ketton]] [[Spy/Legends|spy]] who had taken refuge at the [[Kwenn Space Station/Legends|Kwenn Space Station]]. Max and Katarn succeeded in apprehending their quarry but nearly destroyed the [[Space station/Legends|station]] while doing so. ==Biography== Max was a pint-sized [[bunny]], homicidal and demented by nature. He worked frequently with a [[Unidentified sentient dog|sentient dog]] partner and was considerably dangerous in his work, though he asked for only a small fee. Max's visage could be found in at least two locations: A representation of his head adorned the interior of a [[Buick]] [[Starship/Legends|spacecraft]]''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'' as well as [[Fuel/Legends|fueling]] pipes within [[Fuel City]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]]. In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' Max was staying at the [[House of Max]] in the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Barons Hed]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of Sulon. He was sharing a room with a [[Max's companion|Human female]] and had armed himself with a modified [[Bryar pistol/Legends|Bryar pistol]] that could fire repeating blasts. {{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II}} One [[Standard day/Legends|day]], when Max's companion decided to go to a nearby market, a man named [[Kyle Katarn]] barged into their home. Katarn, a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] agent, was pursuing a personal vendetta against the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] and sought to reach the city's [[Government House (Sulon)|Government House]]. There, the [[Model 88-series administration droid|droid]] [[8t88/Legends|8t88]] was deciphering a map to the [[Valley of the Jedi]], which Katarn's [[Morgan Katarn|late father]] had sworn to protect. Katarn had taken it upon himself to pursue the matter; however, [[Gran/Legends|Gran]] thugs and [[Grave Tuskens|Grave Tusken]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] prowled the streets of Baron's Hed, making Katarn's trip hazardous. Max decided to accompany Katarn on his journey and took his heavily modified pistol to the numerous thugs in a haphazard and frantic manner. Once Katarn reached the Government House, the pair parted ways. {{Endgame}} Katarn and Max later teamed up again to apprehend the [[Ketton/Legends|Ketton]] [[Spy/Legends|spy]] [[Derrida/Legends|Derrida]]. They caught up with her on [[Kwenn Space Station/Legends|Kwenn Space Station]],{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050407a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 1}} located in the outer reaches of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].''[[Star Wars Missions 18: Rogue Squadron to the Rescue]]'' They successfully captured Derrida but nearly destroyed the [[Space station/Legends|space station]] in the process. ==Personality and traits== A highly destructive being, Max suffered from deteriorated cognitive functions. His sentient dog partner considered him to be highly dangerous, and when Max was pursuing Derrida on Kwenn Space Station, his actions, combined with those of ally, Kyle Katarn, nearly razed the installation. Max had [[Color/Legends|white]] [[Hair/Legends|fur]] and brown [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|Another confused census taker?''"
"''Actually, it was the Commissioner with another idiotic and baffling assignment.''"
"''Does it involve wanton destruction?''"
"''We can only hope.|Sam and Max, after receiving a phone call|[[Wikipedia:Sam & Max Hit the Road|''Sam & Max Hit the Road'']]}} [[File:SamAndMaxHitTheRoad.png|thumb|left|250px|LucasArts' 1993 video game ''Sam & Max Hit the Road'']] The character Max belongs to artist [[Steve Purcell]]'s animated media franchise, [[Wikipedia:Sam & Max|''Sam & Max'']], which first found commercial success in [[1987]] as a full-length [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series.{{WebCite|url=http://www.telltalegames.com/summerofsamandmax/history/history1|text=Telltale Games - The Summer of Sam & Max|work=[http://www.telltalegames.com Telltale Games]|archivedate=20130123003356}} Purcell eventually went to work for [[LucasArts]] as an animator and began writing ''Sam & Max'' comics for the LucasArts quarterly magazine ''[[The Adventurer]]'', including several that parodied ''[[Star Wars]]''. In [[1993]], LucasArts released the first ''Sam & Max'' [[Star Wars video games|video game]], [[Wikipedia:Sam & Max Hit the Road|''Sam & Max Hit the Road'']], which Purcell helped design.{{WebCite|url=http://www.telltalegames.com/summerofsamandmax/history/history2|text=Telltale Games - The Summer of Sam & Max|work=[http://www.telltalegames.com Telltale Games]|archivedate=20130502024150}} Max, sometimes accompanied by Sam, has also made [[Easter egg]] cameo appearances in several other LucasArts video games, including [[Wikipedia:Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade: The Graphic Adventure|''Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade: The Graphic Adventure'']], [[Wikipedia:The Secret of Monkey Island|''The Secret of Monkey Island'']], and [[Wikipedia:Day of the Tentacle|''Day of the Tentacle'']], among others.[[Wikipedia:Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade: The Graphic Adventure|''Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade: The Graphic Adventure'']][[Wikipedia:The Secret of Monkey Island|''The Secret of Monkey Island'']][[Wikipedia:Day of the Tentacle|''Day of the Tentacle'']] Max also makes cameos in a number of LucasArts ''Star Wars'' games, including ''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]'' ([[1995]]), in which the outline of Max's head is visible on a map of the [[Ice Station Beta/Legends|Ice Station Beta]] facility;''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]'' [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (video game)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'']] ([[1996]]), which uses Max's likeness to represent a "Challenge Point" icon in the game's [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] level;[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (video game)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' ([[1997]]); and ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'' ([[2003]]), which includes Max's head in the "[[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]]" of the game's un-lockable Buick feature. [[File:Sam_and_Max-Adventurer_4.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Sam & Max parodying ''The Empire Strikes Back'' in ''[[The Adventurer 4]]'']] Yet 1997's ''Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II'' remains Max's most substantial cameo. In the game, Max is featured in two cameos, the lesser appearing in "Level 9: Fuel Station Launch." At the end of the level, the player can see three images of Max's face on a part of Fuel City's docking array. However, the more significant cameo takes place in "Level 5: Baron's Hed – The Fallen City." If the player moves across the lowest step of a staircase in the early stages of the level, a woman in a nearby building will open a door and leave. If moving quickly enough, the player can sneak in through the opened door. If too slow, the player can wait for the woman to return and reopen the door. Within the building, standing on a large chair, is Max, armed with a Bryar pistol. If the player interacts with Max, the bunny will help the player throughout the rest of the level. Should the player fire on Max, the bunny will return fire. Players can also interact with Max once more to activate the "MaxCam," which shows the level from Max's perspective. The [[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II: The Official Strategy Guide|''Dark Forces II'' strategy guide]] later identified Max by name and provided tips to help players find the "Easter egg." Max's physical appearance in the game hews closely to Purcell's original drawings, and his zealous wielding of a proportionally oversized gun is culled directly from the ''Sam & Max'' mythos. ===Canonicity=== {{Quote|Simon the Killer Ewok is about as canon as the hyperkinetic rabbity thing, Max.|Leland Chee|{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=0960#15884843|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2006-11-04|time=12:34 AM|quote=Simon the Killer Ewok is about as canon as the hyperkinetic rabbity thing, Max.}}}} [[File:Icestationbeta_max.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Max's Ice Station Beta cameo in ''Star Wars: Dark Forces'']] Regarding Max's [[canon]]icity within the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity, ''[[The Dark Forces Saga]]'', a series of game scenarios written by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Abel G. Peña]] in [[2005]], identified one of Kyle Katarn's comrades as a "demented, pint-sized [[Rabbit/Legends|lagomorph]]." The same phrase had previously been used to describe Max within ''Sam & Max Hit the Road'', and Peña later confirmed that the lagomorph in question was indeed Max.{{Blog|abelgpena|27|Deconstructing Dark Forces Part 1: The (Pen)Ultimate Easter Egg}} Peña purposely referenced Max in connection to the spy Derrida as a homage to the real-world philosopher [[Wikipedia:Jacques Derrida|Jacques Derrida]], a [[Wikipedia:Deconstructionism|Deconstructionist]]. Peña felt that the message of Deconstructionism as appropriate when referencing a "quasi-canonical Easter egg" like Max because ''Star Wars'' continuity, like reality, is uncertain and will "always be undermined."{{Blog|abelgpena|28|Deconstructing Dark Forces Part 2: The End of Continuity}} When asked about the canon status of [[Simon (Ewok)|Simon the Killer Ewok]] from the [[2001]] video game ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'', [[Leland Chee]], manager of [[Lucasfilm]]'s [[Holocron continuity database]], compared Simon to Max. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Max}} *''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]'' {{1st}} {{So}} *''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces Remaster]]'' {{So}} *''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'' {{C|Inaccessible in normal gameplay}} *[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (video game)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' video game]] {{C|Challenge Point icon}} *''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' ==Sources== *[[The Adventurer 4|''The Adventurer'' 4]] *[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire Limited Collector's Edition|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' Limited Collector's Edition]] {{1stID|Max}} *''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050407a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 1}} *{{Blog|abelgpena|27|Deconstructing Dark Forces Part 1: The (Pen)Ultimate Easter Egg}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{WP|Sam & Max}} *{{Interwiki|samandmax|Sam & Max|Max|Max}} Wiki *{{JCFCite|url=posts/19249124|board=Literature|thread=Kyle Katarn's Tale (Dark Forces Articles by Abel & Jason)|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|date=2005-04-16|time=12:51 PM|archivedate=20180205213413}} {{C|Abel Peña confirms the reference to Max}} {{Interlang |pt=Max (coelho) }} [[Category:Crossover characters]] [[Category:Easter Egg articles]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified gender]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]]
